about: I am a motivated, passionate, and technologically diverse professional seeking a dynamic technical role where I can apply my strategic networking skills to contribute to organizational success. I am driven to enhance network operations and collaborate on innovative solutions that improve network integrity, protect against advanced threats, and ensure regulatory compliance across mission-critical systems.
leadership: With 16 years of experience in the Army, I have developed a leadership style centered on training subordinates, effectively utilizing dynamic technical expertise, employing clear communication, and delivering mission-oriented results. I've had the privilege of training personnel on both network-based and non-network-based programs, ensuring that my subordinates, seniors, and peers are equipped with the skills to handle any technical challenge. As a network engineer, I've successfully led projects that involved systems integration while maintaining security and compliance with regulatory standards. I understand the importance of teamwork and collaboration, and I take pride in mentoring personnel to foster a learning environment where every team member's skill, knowledge, and experience are valued. My leadership is rooted in a deep commitment to continuous learning and improving technological mastery, while always striving to meet the needs of the mission and the organization.
communication: I am adept at translating complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for diverse audiences, including technical teams, leadership, and non-technical stakeholders. By collaborating closely with cross-functional teams, I ensure security initiatives align with organizational objectives and risk management strategies. I have effectively led training sessions in multiple fields, presented in-depth assessments to executives, and facilitated discussions that drive informed decision-making. My ability to simplify complex topics ensures that all parties are equipped to make proactive and informed decisions.
terminalIntro: [H3x-Resume] Ready. Scroll or use sidebar to navigate.

experience.role: Communications Architect / Telecommunications Specialist / Electronics Technician
experience.summary: MOS: 25E Electromagnetic Spectrum Manager, 94E Radio Equipment Repairer. 40–105 hours per week across multiple technical duties spanning network engineering, telecommunications, cyber operations, and electronics maintenance.
highlights: Engineered, designed, implemented, and deployed an IP-based pulse emitter radio mesh network to emulate tactical Army ground force signals, enhancing cyber blue team deception operations
With authorization, planned and executed grey box penetration testing, acquired elevated internal access, and reported findings to drive policy and security improvements
Deployed and managed SIEM solutions using CentOS, Nessus, Splunk, and downtrace sensor integration across 4,000+ systems
Provided Tier-3 network support at the Army Cyber Center Southwest Asia, coordinating regional issues, root cause analysis, and vendor liaison for mission-critical infrastructure
Collaborated on remote nodal network deployment with IPS/IDS sensors, software-defined radio, and enterprise OS virtualization for secure mission-critical connectivity
Diagnosed and resolved network-related software and hardware issues remotely using advanced troubleshooting techniques to minimize downtime and maintain uptime
Managed Active Directory for 2,000 users including RBAC, group policies, two-factor authentication, and network security controls
Ensured compliance with host-nation RF spectrum regulations and telecommunication standards across multi-country point-to-point systems
Engineered and maintained long-distance IP-based communication systems with encryption and network security protocols
Programmed and managed 5,000+ radio and IP-based communication systems across global organizational assets
Developed and led training programs on terrestrial network configurations, system architecture, and secure operational usage
Diagnosed and repaired hardware equipment to ensure operational efficiency using specialized tools aligned with material management and maintenance
Performed system testing, calibration, and component repairs to meet technical and operational standards
Upgraded system hardware and implemented replacements through proactive lifecycle management to meet evolving requirements
